judge.rationale: Decomposition is spot-on — three domains, four port types, internal storage abstraction, state machine, all matching reference structure. Documentation is exceptional (ownership annotations, purpose statements, event flow clarity). However, port interfaces are incomplete: TaskStoragePort and ProjectStoragePort omit `list()` and `delete()` methods needed for realistic project/task queries; ProjectLookupPort is over-engineered with both `projectExists()` and `getProject()` when reference requires only `exists()`. Method naming also diverges (getById vs get). Surface-first approach is correct; ports drive architecture — but the surface itself has gaps before implementation can begin.
